title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the acceleration vector, we need to first determine the net force acting on the object. This can be done by adding the three given force vectors.

title: Calculate the Net Force
text: Add the three force vectors: $$F_1=-3i+2j$$, $$F_2=6.00i-4.00j$$, and $$F_3=2i+5j$$.

title: Relate Force and Acceleration
text: Use Newton's second law, $$F=ma$$, to relate the net force to the acceleration vector.

title: Express Acceleration in Terms of Mass
text: Given that the net force is $$5i+3j$$ and the magnitude of acceleration is $$4.23$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, express the acceleration vector in terms of mass $$m$$.

title: Check Your Units
text: Ensure that the units of the acceleration vector are in $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, which means dividing the force vector by mass $$m$$.
